Curriculum needs to focus on what you can do with information rather than the information itself. I wouldnât eliminate content, without background we cannot form good, rational, and educated conclusions. We are still giving timed tests in math facts! I know thatâs traditional, but it just isnât as important. My era had no calculators and we needed to be proficient at paper and pencil solutions, but now, perfect calculations are available to everyone. We should focus on what kinds of problems can be solved with mathematics. Spelling is equally less important. Vocabulary should be stressed. My sister years ago was taking a test for a personal assistant position where she was asked to type a large number of words per minute error free. She was laughing at such a test. She said no one does that anymore, they just type then run spell check. Technology is changing our NEEDS opinion is that curriculum should reflect our reality. No one gets paid to solve problems that have already been solved. Students should be exposed to solving problems they have not seen before but that can be solve with the content they have learned in class. This will help them develop problem solving strategies they will be able to use the rest of their lives

Overall, the course is good and provides a solid foundation, but more practical exposure and updated content would make it even better. The quality of placements in my college is average. The salary packages are mostly in the average range, with a few students getting better offers based on their skills and performance. The college does provide placement training, including aptitude classes, mock interviews, and basic soft skills development, which helps students prepare for the recruitment process. However, more focus on industry-level training and technical skills would improve placement outcomes. Overall, placements are satisfactory, but students need to put in extra effort on their own to secure better opportunities. Based on my own experience Lady Brabourne College, is basically a mix of versatile aacademics with a very old school vibe. The campus is actually one of the greenest spots in the city. The libraries are genuinely impressive, quiet, well-stocked, and probably the best place to actually get some studying done. Some of the newer parts, like the RC building, even have air-conditioned rooms and smart boards which feel pretty modern. Ã¢ÂÂBut once youÃ¢ÂÂve been there for a semester, the cracks start to show. The science labs are okay for the syllabus, but a lot of the equipment feels like it hasnÃ¢ÂÂt been updated in decades. The gym and the basketball court look good in photos, but in reality, theyÃ¢ÂÂre often overgrown or just poorly maintained, so nobody really uses them. Ã¢ÂÂThe worst part is definitely the washrooms. Most students agree they are in terrible shapeÃ¢ÂÂoften unsanitary, smelly, and just generally neglected. ItÃ¢ÂÂs a huge contrast to the beautiful building outside. Then thereÃ¢ÂÂs the drinking water situation; the filters are constantly breaking down, so you end up having to buy your own bottles. If youÃ¢ÂÂre staying in the hostel, it feels more like a boarding school than a modern college. The lighting is dim, the rooms are dated, and the rules are so strict that you canÃ¢ÂÂt even use basic electronic gadgets. ItÃ¢ÂÂs a great college for the "brand name," but the daily facilities definitely need a major upgrade. Academically, Lady Brabourne College is easily one of the most prestigious spots under Calcutta University. If youÃ¢ÂÂre in the Geography, Economics, or English departments, youÃ¢ÂÂre in good place. The professors are top-tierÃ¢ÂÂmost of them are highly published researchersÃ¢ÂÂand they genuinely care about the subject. The curriculum is rigorous, and the college library is a goldmine for anyone who actually wants to study. Ã¢ÂÂHowever, the academic pressure can be intense. The college is very old-school about rules; if your attendance drops below the 75% mark, they are not lenient at all. It often feels like youÃ¢ÂÂre back in school because of the constant internal assessments and the strict discipline. Ã¢ÂÂThe syllabus is huge, and the professors move fast. If you miss a week of classes because of an illness, good luck catching up. They expect you to be a scholar from day one, which is mentally draining. The internal marking is notoriously stingy. The departments follows a very traditional, strict grading system. It makes it harder to compete with students from private universities who get high scores easily. There are no workshops for modern skills like data analysis or professional writing. If you don't plan on doing a Master's or a PhD, the academic style here feels a bit disconnected from the real world. Most students here are focused on getting their degrees and moving on to Master's programs at top universities like CU, Jadavpur, or even going abroad. If you're looking for a big corporate campus drive, this probably isn't the place. While companies like TCS, Infosys, and Cognizant do visit occasionally, the number of students actually getting placed is very low, often just a handful each year. The placement cell is more about career counseling and workshops than actual job offers. Ã¢ÂÂThe college brand is great for your CV if you're applying for higher studies, but for direct jobs, itÃ¢ÂÂs basically non-existent. YouÃ¢ÂÂre lucky if even two companies show up for your department. Ã¢ÂÂDon't come here expecting a package. The placement cell is pretty inactive and mostly just shares info about government exams or external seminars. You have to find your own internships and jobs off-campus. Ã¢ÂÂIf you're in a general BA or BSc course, there is zero support for placements. The focus is 100% on theory and exams. ItÃ¢ÂÂs frustrating when you see students in other colleges getting at least some basic job offers. Nil

The average placement rate is strong, especially for core and IT-related branches. Overall, the college ensures decent career oppurtunities for students.

College infrastructure reviews generally highlight a mix of modern, well-equipped campuses and others needing maintenance, often focusing on classroom quality, lab facilities, and Wi-Fi availability. Key aspects often evaluated include library resources, hostel conditions, canteen food, and sports facilities, with newer or private colleges often offering better amenities than some older, established institutions. Faculty Expertise: The quality of academics is heavily dependent on the faculty's academic credentials (such as Ph.D. holders) and their years of research or industry experience. Key review points often include how approachable professors are and whether they, rather than Teaching Assistants (TAs), actually lead classes. Placement Percentage: The ratio of students placed versus the total number of students eligible for placements. Reliable institutions typically maintain a rate above 90%. Median Salary vs. Average Salary: The average can be inflated by one or two "crore" packages. The median salary (the middle value of all offers) is a better indicator of what a typical student actually earns. Good

Reviews: Ã¢ÂÂGood learning environment and supportive faculty.Ã¢ÂÂ My overall experience at this college has been positive. The campus has good infrastructure with well-equipped classrooms, computer labs, and a library with sufficient study resources. Wi-Fi is available in most areas and is generally reliable. The faculty members are supportive and explain concepts clearly, which helps in both theory and practical learning. The campus environment is safe and clean, and basic facilities like canteen, sports areas, and common rooms are properly maintained. Overall, it is a good option for students seeking quality management education in Kolkata with decent career opportunities.

Overall, placements are satisfactory and suitable for students who are proactive about their careers.

University/College: JIMS Engineering Management Technical Campus, Greater Noida
Reviews: For practical growth and solid placement opportunities The campus is compact but well-maintained. The computer labs are a major plusÃ¢ÂÂthey are fully air-conditioned, and the PC hardware is actually well-maintained and capable, which is great if you spend a lot of time on systems. The sports and gym facilities are also solid, providing enough equipment if you're serious about your strength training and want to get some heavy squats in after classes. The library is well-stocked, and the canteen is pretty budget-friendly. The teachers are qualified, approachable, and generally supportive. The best part about the academics here is that many professors encourage actual, real-life experience and practical application over just sitting through theoretical lectures or generic communication workshops. The curriculum covers the necessities, but the practical labs are where you actually learn the most. The placement cell is active and brings in a good number of mass recruiters every year, including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and Capgemini. Because of this, the overall placement percentage is decent (usually around 60-70%). However, the average package usually hovers around the 3.5 LPA to 5 LPA mark, mostly for entry-level technical, support, or sales roles. If you are aiming for higher packages (in the 8Ã¢ÂÂ12 LPA range), you will have to rely heavily on off-campus drives or have a standout portfolio, as those premium on-campus offers are limited. The college provides basic support like resume building and mock interviews, but at the end of the day, securing a top-tier package here comes down entirely to your own hustle, practical skill set, and self-preparation. JIMS Noida is a great choice if you are a self-motivated student. The college provides the platformÃ¢ÂÂgood labs, decent placement drives, and supportive facultyÃ¢ÂÂbut your success will depend on how much you hustle outside of the classroom. Don't just rely on the degree; use the facilities to build your own skills."

University/College: Asian Academy of Film and Television, Noida
Reviews: Good college to study game design The infrastructure of the institution presents a mixed experience, with its most noticeable aspects being the small campus and limited canteen facilities. The campus, though compact, offers a cozy and manageable environment where students can easily navigate between classes without long walks. This creates a sense of familiarity and close community, but at the same time, it restricts space for recreational areas, large events, and future expansion. The lack of open areas may make the environment feel slightly crowded during peak hours. The canteen, while functional, is quite small and struggles to accommodate a large number of students, especially during breaks. Seating is limited, often leading to congestion and long waiting times. The variety of food options is modest, and although the quality is satisfactory, there is room for improvement in both menu diversity and service efficiency. Overall, the infrastructure serves basic needs but falls short in providing comfort and convenience on a larger scale. With some expansion and better planning, particularly in dining and open spaces, the campus experience could be significantly enhanced, making it more enjoyable and accommodating for students. The academy excels in game design with industry-standard courses and hands-on learning. Teachers are knowledgeable, supportive, and guide students effectively, helping them build strong skills for real-world game development. Placements depend largely on studentsÃ¢ÂÂ skill levels, with the college offering support and opportunities. However, it is more beneficial for students to actively build skills and secure placements on their own.

University/College: Jindal School of Liberal Arts and Humanities, Sonipat
Reviews: Shaping Future Leaders JSLH has one of the most creative and open environments. The art studios are spacious, and they are designed for both individual and group work. There are dedicated spaces for digital art, photography, and installations, too. Everything is tech-enabled, whether labs or classrooms. The Bachelor of Fine Arts program here mixes creativity with conceptual understanding. The course structure includes painting, digital media, design, art theory, and other interdisciplinary subjects that connect art with psychology or cultural. Professors are easy to approach and give space to explore your own style instead of being forced into on directiona. The university's career services team supports students with internships in galleries and creative studios. Students also get freelance projects or apply to design schools abroad. The school has good connections with industry professionals who often visit for workshops and other seminars.

The yearly fee for my course is approximately 89, 225. This includes tuition fees, registration charges, development fees, examination fees, and other basic academic charges required by the college. The fee structure is mostly fixed for the academic year, but there may be a small increase in the following years depending or university policies. Overall, the fee remains relatively stable compared to many private colleges. Students also have to pay a refundable caution deposit during admission and minor charges for activities, library and examinations during the course. Scholarships are available for students based on merit. Usually, the top three students for every course are given scholarships or fee concessions based on their academic performance. Some government scholarships are also available for students belonging to reserved categories such as SC, ST, EWS and OBC. The college itself provides scholarship to every Top 3 individuals (Yearly). These scholarships help reduce the financial burden for deserving students.

Supportive campus with strong academics and activities Hindu College has a fairly decent infrastructure for its students. The classrooms are well-spaced and ventilated, providing a comfortable environment for learning. The library is stocked with a good collection of books and reference materials for various courses, though it could be updated with more recent editions in some subjects. The campus has basic computer labs and labs for practical subjects, which are functional and serve the studentsÃ¢ÂÂ needs adequately. The college also has proper seating and study areas for students outside of class hours. Canteen and common areas are clean and accessible, giving students space to relax between lectures. While the campus is not very large compared to big universities, it is well-organized and easy to navigate. Overall, the infrastructure is sufficient for academic and co-curricular activities, though there is room for modernization in some areas. I completed my MA in Economics from Hindu College in 1999. The quality of teaching was excellent, with professors being knowledgeable, approachable, and always ready to clarify doubts. The curriculum was well-structured and covered the theoretical and practical aspects of economics thoroughly. The college encouraged analytical thinking and discussions, which helped us understand real-world economic issues better. Assignments, seminars, and occasional workshops added value to our learning. Overall, the academic environment was supportive, and I feel it prepared me well for my career after graduation. During my time at Hindu College, formal placement support was limited, as the focus back then was primarily on academics. Most students found opportunities through personal networking, referrals, and campus announcements. Several alumni secured positions in banks, government departments, and educational institutions after graduation. The college encouraged internships and practical exposure where possible, which helped students build relevant skills for the job market. While there was no dedicated placement cell like in modern times, the faculty were supportive and guided students individually about career options and competitive exams. Overall, the placement experience was adequate for the time, and proactive students were able to leverage the collegeÃ¢ÂÂs academic reputation and alumni network to find good opportunities. Hindu College provides a strong academic foundation and a supportive environment. Students get opportunities to participate in cultural, co-curricular, and academic activities, making it a well-rounded experience.

Reviews: Excellent college I would recommend to join in this college College infrastructure refers to the physical and technological framework that supports academic, administrative, and student life activities. It is a critical factor in a student's educational experience and a college's overall reputation. Increasingly shifting toward smart classrooms equipped with LCD projectors, audio-visual aids, and high-speed Wi-Fi. Academic infrastructure encompasses both the physical facilities and the organizational systems that support teaching, learning, and research in a college setting. While the physical buildings are visible, the "academic" part of infrastructure also includes digital resources, student support services, and quality assurance frameworks. physical books, these serve as hubs for digital journals, e-books, and quiet study zones. Many now provide 24/7 digital access to databases. College placement is the process where institutions partner with corporations to provide job opportunities directly to graduating students. Reviews: Good campus life with supportive academic atmosphere Campus Wi-Fi is reliable during off-peak hours and the college has added charging points in common areas, which is a thoughtful touch. Reviews: Its a decent college The college infrastructure is adequate and fulfills the essential needs of students. Classrooms are functional with sufficient seating, though a few could benefit from better upkeep and updated equipment. The library offers a respectable collection of books and a quiet space for study, but it tends to become crowded during peak hours. Computer labs are accessible, although some systems would benefit from upgrades to meet modern demands. The campus maintains a generally clean environment and provides Wi-Fi, though connectivity can occasionally be inconsistent. Students have access to common areas for relaxation, but these spaces could be enhanced for comfort and engagement. Sports facilities are available but are limited in both variety and maintenance. The college offers a fairly decent academic environment that provides students with a satisfactory learning experience. Most faculty members are knowledgeable and supportive, making an effort to explain concepts clearly. However, the quality of teaching can vary between subjects, as some instructors continue to rely on traditional methods rather than adopting more practical or interactive approaches. The course curriculum is relevant and covers the essential topics required for the degree, but it could benefit from more frequent updates to keep pace with current industry trends. Regular assignments and internal assessments help students stay consistent with their studies, although they sometimes seem more focused on marks than true understanding. Practical sessions and lab work are available and contribute to hands-on learning, but the resources and guidance provided could be improved for a better experience. The college also organizes seminars and guest lectures occasionally, which are helpful but not as frequent as they could be. Overall, the academics are manageable and provide a solid foundation for students. With enhancements to teaching methods and regular curriculum updates, the academic experience has the potential to become significantly better. It was a nice experience in Asansol Engineering college Asansol Engineering College has a moderately good infrastructure with a mix of strengths and limitations. The campus is spread over a decent area and offers facilities like Wi-Fi connectivity, laboratories, library, hostel, canteen, sports grounds, and basic medical services. Many students appreciate the availability of a large library, multiple labs, and campus amenities like gym, playground, and seminar halls. Asansol Engineering College offers academics that are considered decent and structured, especially for students who are self-motivated. The curriculum generally follows the guidelines of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology, which keeps the syllabus updated with basic industry requirements. Faculty members are supportive, though teaching quality may vary across departments. Regular classes, assignments, and internal exams help maintain academic discipline. However, some students feel that practical exposure and industry-oriented learning could be improved. Overall, academics are satisfactory but require extra effort from students for better outcomes. Asansol Engineering College has average placement opportunities, mainly focused on mass recruiting companies. Each year, several students get placed through campus drives, especially from IT and core branches. The college is affiliated with Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of Technology, and the placement cell organizes training sessions like aptitude tests, mock interviews, and soft skills development. Asansol Engineering College has an overall rating of around 3.1 out of 5 based on student reviews. The college offers decent academics and value for money, with supportive faculty and a structured curriculum. Infrastructure and campus life are satisfactory but not outstanding. Placement opportunities are average, mainly with mass recruiters. Overall, it is a suitable choice for budget-conscious students who are ready to put in extra effort for better career growth.
